dialogue.utterance: Dispatch to Unit 3 for an allergic reaction at --Address Redacted--.
Hello ma'am, I'm Alex, an EMT with the rescue squad. Would you mind telling me why you called 911 today?
Sure. The patient has swelling on her bottom lip. It started after she used a strawberry lip balm and had some strawberry jello.
Is the patient alert?
Yes, she's really alert but a bit disoriented.
Can you tell me her name?
Mary… I think.
Great. You did the right thing by calling 911. Can you tell me what city we're in now?
I think it's… Richmond?
Alright. What date is it today?
Hmm… January 9th, I think.
Good job. It helps a lot. Now, can you tell me what happened today?
She used a strawberry lip balm and had some jello. Then her lip got all swollen.
Thank you for that, ma'am. That's very helpful. Let me check her responsiveness one more time.
Ma'am, if I talk to you, can you respond?
Yeah, I'm here. You're kind.
Ma'am, can you squeeze my fingers for me?
Sure.
Perfect, that was great help. No concerning motor response.
I'll take a quick look at her airway for any abnormal signs. It appears patent at this time.
Let me feel her chest movement. Is that okay, ma'am?
Respiratory rate is 20, with equal chest rise and fall, and regular depth.
Chest movement is symmetric. No signs of labored breathing noted.
May I check your pulse for a moment, ma'am?
Pulse is 77, regular, and strong.
Respiratory rate remains 20.
Blood pressure is 150 over 82.
Oxygen saturation is at 95%.

Pulse is steady and has good quality. 30. Primary Assessment; Skin appearance: Color; Partner: Skin is warm, pink, and dry. No hives or rashes. 31. History of Present Illness; S.A.M.P.L.E.: signs and symptoms; EMT: Ma'am, can you tell me if you've had any other symptoms along with the lip swelling?
Just the swelling. She's had no trouble breathing or any other symptoms.
So, when did she come into contact with the strawberry products?
About 30 minutes ago. 35. History of Present Illness; S.A.M.P.L.E.: events leading up to the event; EMT: And the swelling developed shortly after consumption?

dialogue.micro_intent: events leading up to the event
dialogue.utterance: Yes, just after a few bites.
What prescriptions is she on? I see some listed on the nightstand.
Yes, she's currently on quite a few-some for her heart and dementia.
She's on Cozaar, Namenda, Lasix, and other medications for anemia and cholesterol.
Does she have any known medication or food allergies?
Yes. She's allergic to Bactrim, Penicillin, Diclofenac, Augmentin, Hydrochlorothiazide, Prednisone, and Biaxin.
What are her long-term medical conditions?
She has dementia, hypertension, kidney disease stage 3, glaucoma, GERD, and anemia.
She also has hyperlipidemia. That all fits in with her medication list.

Yes, she's been on statins and other medications for cholesterol control. 46. Pain Assessment; O.P.Q.R.S.T.: severity; EMT: Are you in any kind of pain on a scale of 0 to 10 right now, ma'am? 47. Pain Assessment; O.P.Q.R.S.T.: severity; Patient: I'm not hurting at all. It's a 0.

dialogue.role: Presence of petechia, purpura, abrasions; EMT
dialogue.utterance: I'm going to take a closer look at your skin now. Any other swelling besides your lip?

No other signs noted. Just the bottom lip is mildly swollen. 50. Secondary Assessment; head and neck exam: Pupillary reflex / Size of pupils; EMT: Would you let me check your pupils? Just a quick light test.
Pupils are equal and reactive to light. No signs of blockage or injury.
I'm going to take a look in her mouth and down her neck. Is that okay with you?
No embarrassment noted. Oral cavity is clear. No neck masses.
Have you had trouble swallowing at all since the lip swelling?
No, not really.
Any confusion or memory issues today besides what you mentioned earlier?
Conscious, disoriented, and follows single commands. GCS is around 14.
Let me check her blood sugar. Is that okay, ma'am?
The meter isn't giving us anything. Possible equipment issue.
Placing the ECG leads now for a proper cardiac rhythm check.
ECG applied successfully. Rhythm shows sinus with no changes noted.
4 lead ECG monitoring applied per our protocol for allergic reaction cases.
I'm going to ask you to lie still for a few more tests to confirm everything is okay, ma'am.
Second 4 lead monitoring check complete. No arrhythmias detected.
No signs of compromise to the airway, so we don't need immediate airway interventions now.
Third 4 lead monitoring done-again sinus rhythm remains stable.
Family, do you agree with us transporting her to Chippenham Hospital?
Yes, that's what she needs.
Ma'am, we're going to get you on the stretcher now. Let's make sure we keep you comfortable.
She's got the restraint harness secured. All set for transport.
Thank you again for your cooperation. Safety is our top concern here.
All secured and ready. We're all strapped in and ready to move.
Just going to check over the ECG monitoring again for transport prep.
ECG still shows a normal sinus rhythm.
Let me do a quick recheck of the vitals prior to transporting. Pulse is at 82 now.
Blood pressure is 142 over 97.
Oxygen saturation is 97% and pulse remains regular.
Just making sure, is your mother still with the same symptoms?
Yes. Just the swelling on the lower lip, nothing else.

dialogue.role: events leading up to the event; EMT
dialogue.utterance: And she had these exact items you mentioned earlier.

dialogue.role: events leading up to the event; Family Member
dialogue.utterance: Yes, she had a few sips of strawberry jello along with a bit of balm. 82. Reassessments; redo S.A.M.P.L.E.: medications; Partner: All her meds were checked and still in use.
Same documented allergies as before. 84. Reassessments; redo S.A.M.P.L.E.: pertinent past medical history; Family Member: No new conditions have been reported since our first talk.
severity; EMT: What's the current pain level, ma'am?
severity; Patient: Still 0. Just a bit uncomfortable.
All strapped in and secured. Ready for transport to the ED now.
We're staying with the transport plan as discussed with the family.
Partner, we're on protocol 3-3 now for allergic reaction baseline monitoring.
Yep, that's right. ECG and vitals show nothing that requires anaphylaxis emergency steps.
Let me finish checking vitals post-protocol confirmation. Pulse now is 82.
Blood pressure is 142 over 97 and oxygen remains at 97%.
Emergency Department here in the hospital. Let's make sure everything's stable.
Latest vitals are stable before we reach the ED. Pulse of 82, 142/97 BP, 97% oxygen.
Also, her blood pressure briefly dropped to 109 over 65 during our transit. Restabilized after.
Airway and breathing are unremarkable. Stable condition noted throughout the transport.
Everything is set and secured at the hospital. Just finalizing the handoff.
